name: LLM (Simon Willison CLI)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Simon Willison; model plugins may require paid API keys

auth.notes: API keys set via `llm keys set openai` or OPENAI_API_KEY env var; per-provider configuration

performance.notes: LLM provider latency; streaming output supported; logs stored in SQLite

agent_readiness.notes: CLI and Python library for running prompts against LLMs. Plugin system supports 50+ models: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Mistral, Ollama (local), Llama.cpp, Groq, Bedrock, Cohere, etc. Logs all prompts/responses to SQLite. Conversation continuations. Embedding generation and storage. Template system. Python API mirrors CLI. `llm 'prompt' -m claude-3` or `llm logs list` for history. Unix-pipe friendly. Shell scripting with LLMs.

gotchas: v0.x API — still evolving; some plugin APIs change
Plugin quality varies (community plugins less maintained)
SQLite log grows unbounded without pruning
Not designed for high-throughput production use cases
best_when: Shell scripting and CLI workflows with LLMs
Quickly testing prompts across multiple models
Building simple LLM tools with Unix pipes
Local LLM access via Ollama plugin
avoid_when: Production API services (use OpenAI SDK directly)
summary: LLM is Simon Willison's CLI and Python library for running prompts against 50+ LLM providers via plugins (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Groq, Bedrock, etc.). Unix-pipe friendly with streaming output. Logs all conversations to SQLite. Template system and embedding support. Excellent for shell scripting, cross-provider testing, and quick LLM experiments. Python API mirrors the CLI for programmatic use.
